title: Single-cell spatiotemporal dissection of the human maternal–fetal interface
data_sources: 10x Genomics Chromium Next GEM Multiome (Stanford/UCSF tissue banks)

data_scope.spatial_cells: approximately 1.1 million single-cell spatial transcriptomic profiles
data_scope.spatial_sections: 16 second-trimester basal plate sections (GW20-24), each from an independent donor
data_scope.codex_antibodies: 9-antibody panel, representative sections GW15, GW19, GW22
data_scope.tissue_source: Stanford University and UCSF existing placenta tissue banks

core_conclusion: A comprehensive spatiotemporally resolved single-cell multiomic atlas of the human maternal-fetal interface across GW5-39, integrating 191,735 paired snRNA-seq/snATAC-seq nuclei with ~1.1 million submicrometre spatial transcriptomic profiles and CODEX multiplex imaging, reveals novel arterial endothelial state transitions during spiral artery remodelling, a novel DSC4 stromal subtype that suppresses trophoblast invasion via endocannabinoid/CB1 signalling, a LASSO model predicting EVT invasiveness from transcriptomes, and cell-type-resolved genetic risk maps for pre-eclampsia, preterm birth, and miscarriage.
why_it_matters: First gestational-span spatially resolved single-cell multiomic reference of the human placenta and decidua; discovers the DSC4-endocannabinoid axis with cannabis use implications; provides transferable ML invasiveness model validated in independent cohorts; pinpoints convergent POU5F1+LGR5+ endometrial epithelial vulnerability across three major pregnancy complications.
why_nature: Scale exceeds all prior placenta atlases; mechanistic discovery with direct public health stakes (cannabis in pregnancy); simultaneous multi-disease GWAS integration; frontier methodology combining submicrometre spatial transcriptomics, CODEX imaging, GRN reconstruction, pseudotime, ML prediction, and GWAS integration in one study.
added_value_to_my_research: Spatial tissue-community topology (6 niches) -> transferable graph community-detection methods across urban and biomedical spatial data
LASSO iScore with spatial context -> explainable spatially-varying ML predictor, methodological parallel to MGWR/SHAP in health geography
Multi-modal Stereo-seq + CODEX fusion -> encoder-decoder architecture for predicting one spatial modality from another, mirroring geospatial multi-source fusion
Cell foundation model pre-training on 191,735-nuclei atlas -> insights for fine-tuning geospatial foundation models in label-scarce health sensing
SCAVENGE per-cell GWAS risk scores + spatial coordinates -> kernel-density tissue risk maps, conceptually parallel to R2SFCA service-area risk-zone delineation

related.relation: transcription foundation model enabling zero-shot cell annotation and perturbation

related.title: Extravillous trophoblast cell lineage development is associated with active remodeling of the chromatin landscape
related.relation: EVT chromatin remodelling directly parallel to this paper's GRN and GWAS analyses

figures.caption: Single-cell multiomics overview: UMAP of 19 cell types, maternal/fetal origin, GRN rewiring, and bistable toggle-switch trophoblast fate model
Stereo-seq spatial atlas (~1.1 M cells, 16 sections): six recurrent tissue communities, spatial gradients to MFI and vessel walls, arterial endothelial state transitions (caEC->R0->R1->R2)
CODEX multiplex imaging validates stepwise endothelial state transitions (R0/R1/R2) at the protein level during EVT-mediated spiral artery remodelling
Trophoblast developmental trajectories (95,872 trophoblasts), spatial localization of EVT subtypes, pseudovascularization model, and LASSO iScore (n=63,916 EVTs in test set)
Five DSC subtypes (n=20,579), decidualization trajectories, novel DSC4 near anchoring villi, and endocannabinoid/CB1 signalling suppressing EVT invasion
GWAS integration via SCAVENGE: fetal iEVTs enriched for pre-eclampsia; shared POU5F1+LGR5+ endometrial epithelial vulnerability across pre-eclampsia, preterm birth, and miscarriage
